reviews.strongestCounterargument: Cancelling nearly complete children can discard reusable work, defeat request deduplication, reduce cache population, or interrupt effects whose correct recovery costs more than letting them finish.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest initial implication was that every child whose parent expires becomes orphaned; the revision separates sole-consumer work from shared work that retains another live subscriber and value.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Production traces usually omit remote post-cancellation computation and parent-child lifetime joins, so workload fitting requires new telemetry for local release, remote acknowledgement, and billed post-deadline units.
revisions: Added separate liveness and retained-value ledgers so shared deduplicated children are not misclassified as orphan work.
Separated local worker release, remote cancellation acknowledgement, and provider-billed post-deadline computation rather than claiming socket closure stops remote work.
Kept the flat survivor p95 as a negative result and made the joint completion-capacity gate the actual decision surface.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: A task-specific internal benchmark can overfit a small product dataset and miss the broader distribution coverage represented by the provider's multi-benchmark release suite.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest initial implication was that benchmark differences are attributable to weights; the revision labels them provider-reported system results pending exact preprocessing, threshold, suppression, and evaluator replay.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: A decision-grade reproduction needs licensed representative images, blinded adjudication, exact release harness settings, model and runtime revisions, multiple hardware cells, and repeated local-versus-cloud parity runs.
revisions: Added a joint intersection gate measuring correctness, schema validity, and latency on the same examples instead of approving separate dashboards.
Narrowed attribution from model performance to provider-reported system results until preprocessing, thresholds, suppression, and evaluator revisions are replayable.
Made the custom license boundary a first-class architecture constraint rather than treating distribution as conventional open source.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: Browser-local inference replaces a controlled server fleet with a fragmented hardware, driver, browser, thermal, storage, and policy matrix whose qualification cost can exceed the privacy and latency benefit.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest possible claim was that the reported 5-to-60-times range generalizes; the article confines it to Google's tested accelerator-versus-CPU cells on an M4 and refuses cross-runtime ranking.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Representative browser AI evaluation needs physical device coverage, sustained battery and thermal measurement, pinned converted artifacts, cold and warm cache states, browser policies, and reproducible backend selection.
revisions: Made silent CPU fallback a degraded event that must be labeled and rechecked against the latency budget before acceptance.
Added a privacy-minimal coarse capability token and explicit invalidation after browser, runtime, or driver changes.
Separated model-tester graph smoke checks from numeric parity and task-quality evidence on real inputs.
